title: Slice to make a real copy
problem: Make a backup of the scores list, then add 30 to the original — the backup should keep the first two scores.

trace.note: `scores[:]` builds a brand-new list with the same contents. backup and scores are now separate objects — no shared ref id.
trace.checkpoint_prompt: backup was set to scores[:] (a slice). The next line appends 30 to scores. After it runs, what will backup hold?

trace.note: Appending to scores left backup untouched at [10, 20]. To copy a list, slice it (or use list(...)); plain assignment only adds a name.
